entry.title: Improve SolidFire Support Bundle Log Generation and Upload with Automation and OneCollect

link.title: Improve SolidFire Support Bundle Log Generation and Upload with Automation and OneCollect

entry.summary: I don’t have any first hand information to say this is the case, but I believe that with the increased focus on container workloads and container-packaged SolidFire (NetApp eSDS), SolidFire management has slowly been increasingly containerized as well. NetApp Hybrid Cloud Control - a VM with a bunch of management-related containers - is the most prominent example that is familiar to NetApp HCI and SolidFire customers.

entry.title: Faster initialization and rebalancing with Dynamic Disk Pools on NetApp E-Series

link.title: Faster initialization and rebalancing with Dynamic Disk Pools on NetApp E-Series

entry.summary: Here I explained how Dynamic Disk Pools (DDP) work and how they differ from traditional RAID6 (or RAID5) data protection.

entry.title: Data Path in SolidFire clusters with Protection Domains

link.title: Data Path in SolidFire clusters with Protection Domains

entry.summary: In the post that compares SolidFire Protection Domains with Availability Zones I had this illustration of network connectivity among SolidFire v12 cluster nodes and between SolidFire and iSCSI clients.

entry.title: Maximum parallelization of SolidFire Backup and Volume Copy/Clone jobs

link.title: Maximum parallelization of SolidFire Backup and Volume Copy/Clone jobs

entry.summary: SolidFire can backup volumes to S3-compatible Object Storage. How to do it in parallel, I wrote about here.

entry.title: SolidBackup with alternative backup clients
entry.summary: As I mentioned in one of previous posts on the topic of SolidBackup, we first use SolidSync to sync SolidFire Source volume(s) to Clone volume(s), and then SolidBackup generates scripts to run backup off those Clone volume(s). The first example I shared uses Restic, but it can be anything you fancy. How?

entry.title: Role of SolidFire networks in SolidFire storage replication

link.title: Role of SolidFire networks in SolidFire storage replication

entry.summary: Everyone should get the hard-to-find TR-4741 (NetApp Element Software Remote Replication), but to answer the simple question: “all of the above”.

entry.title: Fetch Trident’s SolidFire metrics from outside of your container environment

link.title: Fetch Trident's SolidFire metrics from outside of your container environment

entry.summary: If I had a SolidFire array in a VM and container environment (which I do), I’d prefer to run Prometheus and Grafana in VMs, rather than cram the monitoring into Kubernetes.

entry.title: Monitor SolidFire Network with SolidFire-Exporter, Prometheus and Grafana

link.title: Monitor SolidFire Network with SolidFire-Exporter, Prometheus and Grafana

entry.summary: In the first solidfire-exporter post I went through the first two steps required to gather SolidFire (or NetApp HCI) data and get it into Prometheus. I also said I’ll write additional posts as I find time and opportunity.

entry.title: SolidBackup for data protection and workload mobility for SolidFire in Hybrid Cloud environments

link.title: SolidBackup for data protection and workload mobility for SolidFire in Hybrid Cloud environments

entry.summary: Introduction First attempt Backup and Restore in KVM environments Data Migration Do we really want DIY approaches to protect our data Second attempt Create configuration and keep Src & Dst (Clone) volumes in sync Backup to S3 Restore from S3 Getting there (aka “Digital Transformation”) .snapshot, but on SolidFire Next Steps Conclusion Update (May 30, 2021) SolidSync: PowerShell script that syncs Source to Target volumes SolidBackup: stand-alone PowerShell script that does the following Rinse & reapeat Why not one big script that does it all Demo
